vocabulary.exampleMeaning: I am giving you a book.

vocabulary.meaning: to be pleasing / to like (lit: to please someone)
vocabulary.exampleMeaning: I like music. (lit: Music is pleasing to me.)

vocabulary.meaning: years (genitive plural of год — used for age)
along / by / according to (+ dative in many uses)
vocabulary.exampleMeaning: We are walking along the street.

hook.text: The dative case answers 'to whom?' — it marks the indirect object, the recipient of an action. Give a book to whom? Tell the truth to whom? You also need dative to express age (Мне 20 лет — to me 20 years) and to say you like something (Мне нравится — to me it's pleasing). These three uses alone appear constantly.

why.text: ⚠ Dative is notable because it reverses the English pattern for 'liking'. In Russian, you don't 'like' something — rather, something 'is pleasing to you'. The subject of нравиться is the thing you like, and the person who likes it is in dative. Мне нравится музыка = 'to me music is pleasing'. This structure confuses learners who try to translate literally from English.

cards.title: Dative singular endings
cards.body: Dative singular is regular. Masculine/neuter: add -У/-Ю. Feminine: А→Е, Я→Е, Ь→И.

cards.title: Indirect object — 'to whom'
cards.body: When you give, send, show, or tell something to someone, the recipient is in dative. The direct object (what is given) remains in accusative. Two cases in one sentence.

examples.meaning: I give my friend a book. (другу = dative, книгу = accusative)
Tell me the truth. (мне = to me, dative)
I write my sister a letter. (сестре = dative)

cards.title: Age — мне ... лет
cards.body: To express age in Russian: dative pronoun + number + лет/год(а). The construction is 'to me X years'. лет is genitive plural of год, required after numbers 5 and above. год (one year), года (2-4), лет (5+).

examples.meaning: I am twenty years old. (to me 20 years)
He is thirty years old. (to him 30 years)

cards.title: нравиться — the 'like' structure
cards.body: Russian doesn't have a direct equivalent of 'I like X'. Instead: X нравится мне (X is pleasing to me). The thing liked is the grammatical subject (nominative). The person who likes it is dative. For plural subjects: нравятся. You don't conjugate нравиться to 'I' — you conjugate it to the thing.

examples.meaning: I like music. (lit: music is pleasing to me)
They like books. (books are pleasing to them)

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I am 25 years old'?
questions.explanation: Мне (to me, dative) + двадцать пять (25) + лет (genitive plural of год).

questions.prompt: How does Russian express 'I like music'?
questions.explanation: Both work. Мне нравится = I find it pleasing (dative structure). Я люблю = I love/like (accusative). нравиться is more neutral; любить is stronger.

cards.title: Dative pronouns
cards.body: Personal pronouns have special dative forms that don't follow the noun patterns. Memorise them — they appear in almost every sentence that uses dative.

examples.meaning: to us / to you (pl.) / to them

cards.title: Verbs that take dative
cards.body: Several common verbs always take a dative object (the person affected). помогать (to help), звонить (to call), говорить/сказать (to tell), верить (to believe), советовать (to advise). Learn these as a set — they're triggered by the verb, not by the meaning of the noun.

questions.explanation: сестра → сестре. Feminine А→Е in dative.
сказать (to say) imperative + мне (to me, dative of я). Скажи мне.

questions.prompt: What is 'I like coffee' using нравиться?
questions.explanation: Мне (to me) нравится (is pleasing) кофе (nominative subject).

questions.prompt: How do you say 'He is 30 years old'?
questions.explanation: Ему (to him, dative of он) + тридцать лет.

questions.prompt: I am helping my friend: Я помогаю ___. (друг)
In Мне нравится музыка — what case is мне?
questions.explanation: мне is the dative of я. The structure: [dative person] нравится [nominative thing].

rules: Dative singular: masculine +У/Ю, neuter +У/Ю, feminine А→Е
Indirect object (recipient): Я даю другу книгу (I give a book to my friend)
Age: Мне ... лет (to me X years) — always dative pronoun
⚠ нравиться: thing is subject (nominative), person is dative — reversed from English 'to like'
Key dative verbs: помогать, звонить, говорить/сказать, верить
summary.nextLesson: Next: Instrumental case — with, by means of, and expressing profession.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I like coffee' using нравиться?
What is the dative of я?
I am helping my sister: Я помогаю ___. (сестра)
In Мне нравится музыка, что stands in nominative?
What is the dative of он?
